{{Quote|Remember, you're a soldier, not a murderer. Conduct yourself accordingly.|Captain Beri Tulon to Lieutenant Brenn Tantor on his first day of training|<ref name="Force Commander" />}}
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] '''Beri Tulon''' was an [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. [[Reproduction/Legends|Born]] into a family with a proud military legacy, his traditional principles limited his rise through the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Military rank/Legends|ranks]]. Throughout Tulon's career, he oversaw several battles and took the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Brenn Tantor]] as a protege. After receiving what he considered to be immoral orders from [[Grand General/Legends|Grand General]] [[Malcor Brashin]], Tulon lost confidence in the Empire and ultimately attempted to defect to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]]. While his initial attempt was foiled, he was later rescued by Tantor, who had successfully defected himself. Tulon went on to serve as Tantor's right-hand man in several Rebel missions.

==Biography==
===An officer and a gentleman===
{{Quote|Congratulations, Lieutenant. That's officer thinking.|Beri Tulon to Brenn Tantor|<ref name="Force Commander" />}}
Beri Tulon was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] into a family with a proud military tradition dating back millennia. Family lore stated that his ancestors served with [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]. He happily continued that tradition by entering the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Academy]] as soon as he could. Raised with traditional military values, Tulon was a principled and responsible [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]]. He made a point of earning the friendship and respect of his troops. This caused him to become the target of derision by his peers and superiors, but he was able to back his actions with his excellent results as a [[Commander/Legends|commander]].<ref name="Force Commander" />

[[File:Beri_Tulon.jpg|thumb|left|Beri Tulon as an Imperial officer]]
However, this only served to embitter one of his superiors even more. The resentful [[Officer/Legends|officer]] plotted to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Tulon by sending his unit to [[Khomm/Legends|Khomm]] on what was supposedly a routine reconnaissance mission. To Tulon's surprise, a unit of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] awaited him, slaughtering almost all of his [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. Tulon was able to trace the cowardly attack to the superior and made sure that he faced the full penalty of Imperial justice.<ref name="Force Commander" />

The incident was enough to sour Tulon on command, however. No longer wishing to hold the responsibility for the lives and deaths of his men, he requested that he become a training instructor. Tulon rapidly became one of the most decorated training instructors in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Force Commander" />

Eventually, Tulon became involved in [[Grand General/Legends|Grand General]] [[Malcor Brashin]]'s [[Battlefield Holographic Control Interface]] (BHCI) program. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 ABY]], Tulon, now a [[Captain/Legends|captain]], observed the [[Battle of Kalaan]] to find prospects to be trained on the BHCI. He found [[Brenn Tantor]], a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] corporal who had taken command of his unit when his superior was killed and performed superbly. Striding up to the young man in the ruins of a bombed-out [[Kalaan/Legends|Kalaan]] city, Tulon gave him a battlefield promotion to the rank of [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] and ordered him to report to the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Inquisitor (Star Destroyer)|''Inquisitor'']] for training.<ref name="Force Commander" />

[[File:BrennTantorAndBeriTulonAtBHCI-FC.jpg|thumb|250px|Tulon introduces Lieutenant Brenn Tantor to the BHCI.]]
In orbit over [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], Tulon introduced Tantor to the Battlefield Holographic Control Interface, a command station that allowed an officer to direct troops from the safety of orbit with the fullest possible representation of the actual field of battle. Tulon instructed Tantor as the new officer ran [[Zeta Squadron (Desert Sands)|Zeta Squadron]] through a series of exercises. However, the training was interrupted by the announcement that an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']] containing [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] to the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] had landed somewhere near Zeta Squadron's position. Tulon observed as Tantor swiftly found the pod.<ref name="Force Commander" />

The young officer next tracked down the [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]] that had picked up the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] carrying the plans and commanded a raid on [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] [[Ben Kenobi's home/Legends|hut]]—where the droids had visited—all under Tulon's supervision.<ref name="Force Commander" />

Tantor, Tulon, and Brashin were then sent to [[Ruul (planet)|Ruul]], based on the possibility that the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], carrying the droids and the Death Star plans, may have escaped to there instead of the suspected target of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], which lay on a similar heading. Once there, they discovered that while the ''Millennium Falcon'' had not jumped there, the [[Ruul computer complex|primary computer facility]] on the planet had been seized by forces of the Rebel Alliance. Tulon watched proudly as Tantor, now a captain as well, [[Assault on Ruul|destroyed]] the Rebel presence on Ruul.<ref name="Force Commander" />

===A loss of confidence===
{{Quote|I've labored too long as an officer, following orders that I didn't believe in. Murdering civilians. Destroying cities. Whether I'm a traitor or hero will ultimately be decided by who wins this war.|Beri Tulon to Brenn Tantor upon his attempted defection|<ref name="Force Commander" />}}
This was the last mission in which Tulon supervised Tantor. Tulon, who had long considered himself a man of principle, had lost the confidence of Grand General Brashin. In Brashin's opinion, Tulon's conscience, and consequent instillment of more traditional military values in Tantor, was dangerous and unbecoming in an Imperial officer. Tulon was removed as Tantor's training officer and reassigned.<ref name="Force Commander" />

For half of the following three [[Standard year/Legends|years]], Tulon was assigned to the [[Trasse Proving Grounds]], an Imperial facility that manufactured [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]]. There, the walkers were tested on Rebel prisoners and armored units. Such an assignment only deepened Tulon's discontent with his government. He had been raised by his family to be a man of honor, and as such, he could not make his values fit with those of an Empire that encouraged brutality and injustice. Tulon resolved that he could no longer serve the Galactic Empire.<ref name="Force Commander" />

[[File:Tulon_squad.jpg|thumb|250px|Tulon's task force for the [[Massacre on Abridon]]]]
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Tulon, by now a [[Colonel/Legends|colonel]], was assigned to command a [[Tulon's task force|detachment]] participating in the assault on a Rebel base on [[Abridon/Legends|Abridon]]. Intending to defect on this mission, Tulon learned that his commander would be his old student, Brenn Tantor, now a very high-ranking Imperial [[General/Legends|general]]. Shortly after the [[Uprising on Abridon|mission]] began, Tulon's personal AT-AT, the ''[[Annihilator 1]]'', fired on the rest of his detachment and made a beeline for the Rebel base. Tantor, believing that his mentor was dead or the hostage of renegade elements, pursued the walker and brought it down. He was utterly shocked to find that Tulon had in fact been the one defecting. Tulon attempted to explain to Tantor that the Empire was corrupt and evil, but Tantor, feeling betrayed, and with Brashin monitoring, was unable or unwilling to aid Tulon.<ref name="Force Commander" />

===Fighting for his principles===
{{Quote|I never thought I'd see you again, General.|Beri Tulon to Brenn Tantor upon his rescue|<ref name="Force Commander" />}}
Tulon was sentenced to imprisonment in [[Tarkin Detention Facility]] on [[Ruul (planet)|Ruul]]. However, he never made it there. Unbeknownst to him, Tantor himself, tortured by his own Imperial demons, had refused an order to massacre civilians shortly after Tulon was captured. Tantor arranged his own defection with Rebel General [[Tyr Taskeen]]. Tantor's first mission after his defection was the rescue of Tulon.<ref name="Force Commander" />

[[File:Tulon_to_prison.jpg|thumb|left|Imperial walkers and stormtroopers escort Beri Tulon to Tarkin Detention Facility.]]
Tantor led several [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|troopers]] on a daring [[Mission to Ruul (Galactic Civil War)|commando mission]], destroying the Imperial convoy escorting Tulon across Ruul's surface and spiriting Tulon away in a [[Hover Transport TC9|Hover Transport]]. Tulon learned that Tantor intended to capture an AT-AT walker to prove his loyalty and reliability to Taskeen and needed Tulon's experience with the Trasse Proving Grounds. Tulon realized that they would require a security code to access Trasse undetected and convinced Tantor to raid the Imperial [[Sounder Flats computer complex|computer facility]] at [[Sounder Flats]] in order to gain the codes. Tulon successfully extracted the codes, despite an Imperial counterattack.<ref name="Force Commander" />

In preparation for the [[Mission to Trasse|Trasse operation]], Tulon trained a unit of elite soldiers, known as the [[Infiltrator (armor)|Infiltrators]], to hijack Imperial walkers. They were successfully deployed on [[Trasse/Legends|Trasse]] and captured an AT-AT, but matters were complicated when ''Inquisitor'' appeared in the system, with Grand General Brashin aboard. Tantor's forces crushed Imperial ground opposition in order to rush Tulon to a [[planetary ion cannon]], from which he disabled the Star Destroyer. While accessing the ion cannon's computer banks, Tulon also found some interesting data, suggesting that the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] was nearly operational.<ref name="Force Commander" />

Later, at the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|Liberation of Coruscant]], Tulon was present when Taskeen briefed Tantor for the invasion of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], throne-world of the Empire. Tulon reported [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron's]] successful sabotage of the [[Planetary shield/Legends|shields]].<ref name="Force Commander" />

==Personality and traits==
Beri Tulon was raised in a military family, and was thus brought up with traditional martial values. These would have served him well had the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] continued, but the imposition of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] brought a new system into play.<ref name="Force Commander" />

Strictly traditionalist and raised with a strong sense of morality, Tulon was not cut out for the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. Even so, Tulon was little different from thousands of other Republican-minded officers. However, his placement under a fanatic supporter of the new ideology in the form of Grand General Brashin brought that conflict to the front.<ref name="Force Commander" />

Tulon's reassignment affected his career little, as seen by his promotions, but his new duties appeared to have a much greater impact on his conscience. The brutality that he witnessed and was forced to participate did not inure him to it but instead drove him further from the Empire. Though he valued loyalty, he valued his other principles more and cast his lot in with the Alliance.<ref name="Force Commander" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Beri Tulon was created for ''[[Star Wars: Force Commander]]'', in which he was voiced by [[Peter Firth]].<ref name="Force Commander" />
